daily_brief: A two-week ceasefire between the United States and Iran took effect early Wednesday, averting what President Trump had threatened would be the destruction of Iranian 'civilisation' [BBC, France 24, Globe and Mail]. The deal, mediated by Pakistan, requires Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz to commercial shipping while negotiations begin Friday in Islamabad on a potential permanent settlement. Tehran has proposed a 10-point plan that includes continued uranium enrichment and the lifting of all sanctions—terms Washington has previously rejected [The Hindu, Al Jazeera]. Markets responded with relief: oil prices fell approximately 13-15%, while Asian stocks surged [BBC]. However, Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu clarified the ceasefire does not extend to operations against Hezbollah in Lebanon [France 24, Globe and Mail]. Separately, Vice President JD Vance visited Budapest to endorse Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán ahead of Sunday's election, accusing the EU of 'foreign election interference' [BBC Europe, RFI]. French hostages Cécile Kohler and Jacques Paris departed Iran after more than three years of detention, travelling to Azerbaijan en route to France [BBC Europe, RFI].

top_stories.headline: US and Iran Agree to Two-Week Ceasefire, Averting Threatened Escalation
bullets: President Trump announced the ceasefire approximately 90 minutes before his 8 PM EDT deadline, agreeing to suspend US attacks on Iran for two weeks contingent on Iran reopening the Strait of Hormuz to commercial shipping
Iran issued a 10-point plan for permanent peace that includes continued Iranian control of the Strait of Hormuz, acceptance of its uranium enrichment program, and lifting of all US sanctions
Pakistan mediated the agreement; negotiations are scheduled to begin Friday in Islamabad
Both sides claimed victory: Trump called it a 'total and complete victory' while Iran's Supreme National Security Council declared the US had suffered 'an undeniable, historic and crushing defeat'

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Emphasized Trump's de-escalation from extreme rhetoric; noted deal allows Trump to avoid 'treacherous choice'

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Highlighted Iran's 10-point plan details and both sides claiming victory

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Focused on political implications for Trump including midterm election concerns and MAGA movement fractures

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Emphasized Iran's demands including uranium enrichment acceptance

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Noted Democratic calls for accountability over 'illegal war' alongside Republican scepticism

top_stories.headline: Oil Prices Plunge, Global Markets Rally on Iran Ceasefire News
bullets: Brent crude fell approximately 13% to $94.80 per barrel; US-traded oil dropped more than 15% to $95.75
Asian stock markets surged: Japan's Nikkei gained 5%, South Korea's Kospi jumped nearly 6%, Hong Kong's Hang Seng rose 2.8%
Oil prices remain significantly elevated from pre-war levels of approximately $70 per barrel on February 28
Madagascar declared a 15-day state of energy emergency due to supply disruptions from the conflict

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Straightforward market reporting with regional breakdown

sources.name: Financial Times
sources.framing_note: Brief headline treatment focused on ceasefire announcement

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Highlighted Madagascar's energy emergency as example of global impact

top_stories.headline: Vice President Vance Endorses Orbán in Budapest, Accuses EU of Election Interference
bullets: JD Vance visited Budapest five days before Hungary's April 12 election, stating he was there 'to help' Orbán's campaign
Vance accused EU 'bureaucrats' of 'one of the worst examples of foreign election interference' he had ever seen
Orbán faces his toughest electoral challenge in 16 years, with rival Péter Magyar leading in most opinion polls
This marks the first top-level US visit to Hungary in 20 years

sources.name: BBC Europe
sources.framing_note: Provided extensive context on Orbán's political record and EU tensions; noted Magyar's poll lead

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Emphasized Vance's role as supporter of European far-right parties

top_stories.headline: French Hostages Kohler and Paris Leave Iran After Three Years in Detention
bullets: Cécile Kohler, 41, and Jacques Paris, 72, departed Iran in a diplomatic convoy with the French ambassador on Tuesday
The couple were arrested in May 2022 during what their families described as a tourist trip; they were convicted of espionage charges France called baseless
They were released from Evin prison in November 2025 and had been under house arrest at the French embassy since then
President Macron thanked Oman for mediation efforts; the couple traveled to Azerbaijan en route to France

sources.name: BBC Europe
sources.framing_note: Detailed timeline of detention and release process

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Emphasized French Foreign Minister's weekend discussions with Iranian counterpart

sources.name: Le Monde
sources.framing_note: Article inaccessible; headline referenced Iran's 'tactical gesture'

top_stories.headline: Kanye West Blocked from UK Entry, Wireless Festival Cancelled
bullets: British officials refused West entry on grounds his presence would not be 'conducive to the public good' following his repeated antisemitic remarks
Major sponsors Pepsi and Diageo had already withdrawn from the three-day festival scheduled for July 10-12
Prime Minister Keir Starmer said West 'should never have been invited' and pledged to 'confront and defeat the poison of antisemitism'
Presale tickets had sold out at midday Tuesday before the cancellation was announced

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Comprehensive coverage including sponsor withdrawals and government statements

sources.name: RNZ
sources.framing_note: Shorter report focusing on government decision and Starmer's statement

Burkina Faso's military ruler Ibrahim Traoré tells citizens to 'forget about democracy' in state TV interview

One gunman killed and two injured in shooting near Israeli consulate in Istanbul; Turkish president calls it 'heinous act of terror'
US Justice Department disrupts Russian military-run DNS hijacking network operated by GRU Unit 26165
Former French President Sarkozy tells appeals court 'I am innocent' in Libya campaign funding case
Train driver killed after high-speed passenger train collides with lorry carrying military equipment in northern France
Cyclone Maila upgraded to Category 4, causing flooding and infrastructure damage in Papua New Guinea and Solomon Islands
Iran and China use Strait of Hormuz crisis to promote yuan as alternative to US dollar in oil transactions
